ops.title: Run a plugin from the local repo via `claude plugin marketplace add <path>` — but the cache is version-key-gated, so same-version edits never propagate; force with uninstall+install
ops.body:
To run a plugin's working-tree code instead of the GitHub-installed copy, use the fleet's documented flow (cc-skills README §"Hack on a local checkout"): `claude plugin marketplace add ~/Code/cc-skills` re-points the `skills` marketplace at the clone, then `claude plugin update @skills` copies the working tree (uncommitted files included) into `~/.claude/plugins/cache/` — but ONLY when plugin.json's version differs from the installed key. **Why:** the plugin cache is version-key-gated. `claude plugin update` at an unchanged version is a silent no-op, so post-copy edits never propagate and you test stale code. **How to apply:** bump plugin.json first (the pending release bump usually suffices); to re-copy at the same version, `claude plugin uninstall

@` + `claude plugin install

@` (a fresh install always copies). For rapid iteration, `claude --plugin-dir ` + `/reload-plugins` bypasses the cache entirely. ALWAYS restore the GitHub marketplace after (`claude plugin marketplace add yasyf/cc-skills`) — a marketplace left on a local path silently stops tracking GitHub. Related: [[cookiesync-web-storage]].
